description: Cross-cutting security boundaries, privacy-aware events/diagnostics, fixture coverage, validation gates, rollout phases, and rollback behavior for Workflow System V1.

userStories.title: Enforce workflow security boundaries
userStories.description: As a security reviewer, I want workflows to obey existing Pibo boundaries so that workflows do not create a privileged path.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation, capability validation, executor policy resolution, and runtime execution enforce session, tool, compute-worker, auth, profile, project ownership, and environment policies
Code nodes and adapters use registered trusted handlers only
Security tests verify workflows cannot bypass profile, tool, skill, context, session routing, or compute-worker policies
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement structured diagnostics and error families
userStories.description: As a developer, I want diagnostics and errors to be actionable so that implementation failures are easy to fix.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow diagnostic shape includes code, message, optional path, nodeId, edgeId, severity, and hint
Error families include definition, graph, interface, execution, retry-exhausted, adapter, and node-executor errors
Diagnostics reveal enough to fix errors without leaking hidden payloads in normal UI
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Emit minimum workflow event contract
userStories.description: As an operator, I want workflow records and events to reconstruct current run status for CLI and Chat Web.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow events include workflow started, workflow waiting, workflow completed, workflow failed, node started, node completed, node failed, edge transferred, and checkpoint created at minimum
Events drive trace, UI, CLI/debug, XState projection, and audit views
Unauthorized data is not exposed through event projections
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add required workflow fixtures
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want deterministic fixtures so that the implementation can be verified repeatably.
acceptanceCriteria: Fixtures include one-node agent, mixed nodes, adapter, human wait, registry/plugin, debug serialization, nested workflow, and bounded review loop
Fixtures cover all node kinds, port types, adapters, guards, joins, state policies, loops, waits, and registry refs
Fixture snapshots are deterministic where serialization or projection is expected
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add validation and persistence test suites
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want automated tests so that V1 runtime invariants are protected.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation suite accepts valid fixtures and rejects malformed definitions with structured diagnostics
Persistence suite covers completed, failed, waiting, resumed, retry-scheduled, cancelled, nested child failure, stale lease reclaim, and restart-after-boundary cases
AI-output eval verifies schema-bound Agent outputs validate or fail before completion/downstream transfer
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add manual release validation checklist
userStories.description: As an operator, I want manual checks before rollout so that core user flows are verified end to end.
acceptanceCriteria: Manual checklist includes npm run typecheck, workflow tests, persistence tests, one-node pibo-agent workflow, adapter composition workflow, and bounded review loop
Manual results are recorded in validation report or equivalent release artifact
No V1 release is considered complete until all checks pass
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement workflow execution rollback switch
userStories.description: As an operator, I want rollback to preserve normal sessions so that workflow failures do not break core Pibo usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Feature flag or equivalent operational switch disables workflow execution
Rollback ignores workflow metadata in session routing while preserving normal Pibo Sessions
Persisted workflow records remain inspectable after rollback
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document rollout phases and V1 non-goals
userStories.description: As an engineering lead, I want clear gates and scope boundaries so that implementation does not creep.
acceptanceCriteria: Rollout gates cover MVP, v1.1, v1.2, and v2.0 or equivalent feature-completeness phases
Docs state V1 excludes new cross-user permission model, general audit/compliance product, production distributed scheduler readiness, marketplace approval/sandboxing, and full visual editor
Docs live under docs/ according to project documentation structure
Typecheck passes
